1.A.J. EwingLHBNYM vs Jake Irvin (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.397 / xBA 0.267 / xSLG 0.525 (+82 pts)Park +24 pts14d 0.431vs RHP —K 16.7%BB 12.9%HR 10.3%Hit 21.4%Edge Hitter

A.J. Ewing LHB vs Jake Irvin RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 115+0+0, pitcher rows 795+2947+3050

Verdict

Irvin's sinker is the projected matchup advantage, while A.J. Ewing should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.397 xwOBA (+82 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +7 pts (raw 0.366 -> adj 0.373); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Jake Irvin is in a 14-day rough patch (+108 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. 2.Juan SotoLHBNYM vs Jake Irvin (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.478 / xBA 0.296 / xSLG 0.613 (+163 pts)Park +37 pts14d 0.315vs RHP 0.356K 14.1%BB 16.0%HR 10.3%Hit 26.2%Strong Hitter

Juan Soto LHB vs Jake Irvin RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 498+3015+3255, pitcher rows 795+2947+3050

Verdict

Irvin's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Juan Soto should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.478 xwOBA (+163 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +20 pts (bb_type-adj 0.421 -> final 0.478); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Juan Soto is on a 14-day slump (-63 pts vs season). Jake Irvin is in a 14-day rough patch (+108 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

3.Cal RaleighLHBSEA vs Noah Schultz (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.322 / xBA 0.245 / xSLG 0.369 (+7 pts)Park -23 pts14d 0.148vs LHP —K 30.3%BB 12.7%HR 10.2%Hit 12.8%Even

Cal Raleigh LHB vs Noah Schultz LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 778+3098+2531, pitcher rows 491+0+0

Verdict

Schultz's cutter is the projected matchup advantage, while Cal Raleigh should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.322 xwOBA (+7 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -25 pts (raw 0.328 -> adj 0.304); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +41 pts (bb_type-adj 0.304 -> final 0.322); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Cal Raleigh is on a 14-day slump (-142 pts vs season). Noah Schultz is in a 14-day rough patch (+115 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Sweeper39.20.3110.325GB 42% / LD 24% / FB 27% / PU 7%0.301-100.3110.2440.38329.1

4-Seam Fastball11.30.3240.319GB 42% / LD 24% / FB 27% / PU 7%0.302-220.3060.2400.38222.1

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

4.Kyle SchwarberLHBPHI vs Nick Lodolo (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.364 / xBA 0.259 / xSLG 0.404 (+49 pts)Park -4 pts14d 0.434vs LHP —K 24.1%BB 10.7%HR 8.1%Hit 17.5%Slight Hitter

Kyle Schwarber LHB vs Nick Lodolo LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 881+3157+2950, pitcher rows 165+2497+1865

Verdict

Lodolo's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Kyle Schwarber should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.364 xwOBA (+49 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -21 pts (raw 0.347 -> adj 0.326); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +41 pts (bb_type-adj 0.326 -> final 0.364); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

5.Wilyer AbreuLHBBOS vs Seth Lugo (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.378 / xBA 0.284 / xSLG 0.439 (+63 pts)Park +17 pts14d 0.326vs RHP 0.351K 22.4%BB 9.0%HR 7.8%Hit 23.6%Edge Hitter

Wilyer Abreu LHB vs Seth Lugo RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 731+1641+1806, pitcher rows 851+2381+3285

Verdict

Lugo's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Wilyer Abreu should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.378 xwOBA (+63 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +9 pts (raw 0.338 -> adj 0.347); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +14 pts (bb_type-adj 0.347 -> final 0.378); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Seth Lugo is in a 14-day rough patch (+92 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

6.Kyle HigashiokaRHBTEX vs Jose Quintana (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.338 / xBA 0.255 / xSLG 0.411 (+23 pts)Park +4 pts14d —vs LHP —K 14.9%BB 6.7%HR 7.7%Hit 20.9%Even

Kyle Higashioka RHB vs Jose Quintana LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 302+1280+1143, pitcher rows 562+2275+3093

Verdict

Quintana's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Kyle Higashioka should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.338 xwOBA (+23 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -16 pts (raw 0.334 -> adj 0.318); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +16 pts (bb_type-adj 0.318 -> final 0.338); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Jose Quintana is in a 14-day hot stretch (-50 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

7.Tyler O'NeillRHBBAL vs Shane McClanahan (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.306 / xBA 0.243 / xSLG 0.343 (-9 pts)Park -20 pts14d 0.282vs LHP —K 31.0%BB 9.7%HR 7.5%Hit 16.4%Even

Tyler O'Neill RHB vs Shane McClanahan LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 331+827+2003, pitcher rows 629+0+0

Verdict

McClanahan's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Tyler O'Neill should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.306 xwOBA (-9 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -23 pts (raw 0.313 -> adj 0.289); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +36 pts (bb_type-adj 0.289 -> final 0.306); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Shane McClanahan is in a 14-day hot stretch (-65 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Changeup35.20.3060.276GB 59% / LD 20% / FB 16% / PU 4%0.283-230.2430.2160.25657.9

Curveball13.90.3150.299GB 46% / LD 19% / FB 30% / PU 4%0.309-60.2940.2320.36638.0

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

9.Munetaka MurakamiLHBCWS vs Bryan Woo (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.267 / xBA 0.243 / xSLG 0.394 (-48 pts)Park -23 pts14d 0.383vs RHP 0.413K 25.9%BB 8.4%HR 6.8%Hit 14.2%Slight Pitcher

Munetaka Murakami LHB vs Bryan Woo RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 863+0+0, pitcher rows 770+2784+1717

Verdict

Woo's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Munetaka Murakami should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.267 xwOBA (-48 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -19 pts (raw 0.324 -> adj 0.305); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -14 pts (bb_type-adj 0.305 -> final 0.267); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Bryan Woo is in a 14-day hot stretch (-109 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Bat-tracking interaction

bat speed 73.6 mph
swing length 7.8 ft
attack angle 18.3°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-3.7+15.749+12.0swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Sinker-5.0+16.012+10.9swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Changeup-6.5+21.524+15.1swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Slider-6.7+21.723+15.0swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Sweeper-6.9+20.112+13.2swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

10.Kyle ManzardoLHBCLE vs Framber Valdez (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.315 / xBA 0.229 / xSLG 0.310 (+0 pts)Park -4 pts14d 0.458vs LHP —K 26.2%BB 5.6%HR 6.8%Hit 20.6%Even

Kyle Manzardo LHB vs Framber Valdez LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 599+2074+709, pitcher rows 800+2898+2683

Verdict

Valdez's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Kyle Manzardo should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.315 xwOBA (+0 pts vs league, Even). GB-mix adjustment: -34 pts (raw 0.301 -> adj 0.267); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +52 pts (bb_type-adj 0.267 -> final 0.315); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Kyle Manzardo is on a 14-day heater (+133 pts vs season). Framber Valdez is in a 14-day rough patch (+66 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

11.James WoodLHBWSH vs Christian Scott (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.435 / xBA 0.272 / xSLG 0.482 (+120 pts)Park +26 pts14d 0.455vs RHP 0.504K 28.6%BB 14.3%HR 6.4%Hit 26.2%Strong Hitter

James Wood LHB vs Christian Scott RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 928+2923+1406, pitcher rows 288+0+788

Verdict

Scott's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while James Wood should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.435 xwOBA (+120 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +51 pts (bb_type-adj 0.358 -> final 0.435); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

13.Aaron JudgeRHBNYY vs Patrick Corbin (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.407 / xBA 0.273 / xSLG 0.437 (+92 pts)Park -13 pts14d 0.342vs LHP —K 17.7%BB 11.1%HR 6.1%Hit 26.2%Edge Hitter

Aaron Judge RHB vs Patrick Corbin LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 891+2875+3235, pitcher rows 539+2590+2889

Verdict

Corbin's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Aaron Judge should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.407 xwOBA (+92 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -24 pts (raw 0.360 -> adj 0.336); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +84 pts (bb_type-adj 0.336 -> final 0.407); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Aaron Judge is on a 14-day slump (-82 pts vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Split-Finger0.00.3180.342GB 41% / LD 27% / FB 26% / PU 6%0.291-270.3180.2740.40760.8

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Royce Lewis RHB vs Tatsuya Imai RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 462+1518+1242, pitcher rows 286+0+0

Verdict

Imai's sinker is the projected matchup advantage, while Royce Lewis should look to drive the slider. Projection: 0.338 xwOBA (+23 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +23 pts (raw 0.310 -> adj 0.332); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +13 pts (bb_type-adj 0.332 -> final 0.338); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

15.Shohei OhtaniLHBLAD vs Michael King (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.368 / xBA 0.254 / xSLG 0.474 (+53 pts)Park -23 pts14d 0.377vs RHP 0.438K 25.7%BB 12.7%HR 5.9%Hit 20.8%Edge Hitter

Shohei Ohtani LHB vs Michael King RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 777+3241+3152, pitcher rows 831+1238+2993

Verdict

Projection: 0.368 xwOBA (+53 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -21 pts (raw 0.366 -> adj 0.345); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +46 pts (bb_type-adj 0.345 -> final 0.368); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

16.Mickey MoniakLHBCOL vs MacKenzie Gore (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.371 / xBA 0.269 / xSLG 0.446 (+56 pts)Park +13 pts14d 0.269vs LHP —K 29.4%BB 7.8%HR 5.9%Hit 25.7%Edge Hitter

Mickey Moniak LHB vs MacKenzie Gore LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 549+1602+1534, pitcher rows 836+2819+2995

Verdict

Gore's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Mickey Moniak should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.371 xwOBA (+56 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +30 pts (raw 0.310 -> adj 0.340); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +18 pts (bb_type-adj 0.340 -> final 0.371); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Mickey Moniak is on a 14-day slump (-65 pts vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. 17.Colson MontgomeryLHBCWS vs Bryan Woo (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.297 / xBA 0.221 / xSLG 0.391 (-18 pts)Park -21 pts14d 0.334vs RHP 0.366K 26.3%BB 6.5%HR 5.9%Hit 19.2%Even

Colson Montgomery LHB vs Bryan Woo RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 688+1088+0, pitcher rows 770+2784+1717

Verdict

Woo's 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Colson Montgomery should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.297 xwOBA (-18 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -7 pts (raw 0.302 -> adj 0.295); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +23 pts (bb_type-adj 0.295 -> final 0.297); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Bryan Woo is in a 14-day hot stretch (-109 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

19.Pete Crow-ArmstrongLHBCHC vs Brandon Sproat (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.395 / xBA 0.264 / xSLG 0.454 (+80 pts)Park +36 pts14d 0.353vs RHP 0.336K 22.8%BB 8.5%HR 5.8%Hit 25.1%Edge Hitter

Pete Crow-Armstrong LHB vs Brandon Sproat RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 689+2403+1424, pitcher rows 616+291+0

Verdict

Sproat's cutter is the projected matchup advantage, while Pete Crow-Armstrong should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.395 xwOBA (+80 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +17 pts (raw 0.324 -> adj 0.341); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +18 pts (bb_type-adj 0.341 -> final 0.395); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

20.George SpringerRHBTOR vs Ryan Weathers (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.302 / xBA 0.230 / xSLG 0.396 (-13 pts)Park -7 pts14d 0.224vs LHP —K 22.5%BB 9.0%HR 5.7%Hit 18.0%Even

George Springer RHB vs Ryan Weathers LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 445+2580+2307, pitcher rows 732+642+1417

Verdict

Weathers' sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while George Springer should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.302 xwOBA (-13 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -24 pts (raw 0.325 -> adj 0.301); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +8 pts (bb_type-adj 0.301 -> final 0.302); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. George Springer is on a 14-day slump (-50 pts vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

22.Jake BurgerRHBTEX vs Jose Quintana (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.378 / xBA 0.280 / xSLG 0.455 (+63 pts)Park +11 pts14d 0.362vs LHP —K 14.4%BB 7.4%HR 5.5%Hit 25.8%Edge Hitter

Jake Burger RHB vs Jose Quintana LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 638+1324+2089, pitcher rows 562+2275+3093

Verdict

Quintana's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Jake Burger should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.378 xwOBA (+63 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +16 pts (raw 0.331 -> adj 0.346); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +21 pts (bb_type-adj 0.346 -> final 0.378); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Jake Burger is on a 14-day heater (+80 pts vs season). Jose Quintana is in a 14-day hot stretch (-50 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

bat speed 73.3 mph
swing length 7.4 ft
attack angle 7.7°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
Sinker-5.8+0.322-5.6swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk
Changeup-7.9+13.444+5.6swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Curveball-9.6+14.516+4.9swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
4-Seam Fastball-4.6+2.466-2.2on plane
Slurve-8.9+7.7*1-1.1on plane

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

23.Riley GreeneLHBDET vs Slade Cecconi (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.372 / xBA 0.257 / xSLG 0.478 (+57 pts)Park +1 pts14d 0.317vs RHP 0.334K 24.9%BB 7.4%HR 5.4%Hit 26.8%Edge Hitter

Riley Greene LHB vs Slade Cecconi RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 803+2826+2545, pitcher rows 776+2116+1298

Verdict

Cecconi's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Riley Greene should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.372 xwOBA (+57 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -12 pts (raw 0.353 -> adj 0.341); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +31 pts (bb_type-adj 0.341 -> final 0.372); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Riley Greene is on a 14-day slump (-59 pts vs season). Slade Cecconi is in a 14-day hot stretch (-47 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

24.Michael Harris IILHBATL vs Max Meyer (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.360 / xBA 0.269 / xSLG 0.461 (+45 pts)14d 0.276vs RHP 0.311K 22.5%BB 3.6%HR 5.4%Hit 28.5%Slight Hitter

Michael Harris II LHB vs Max Meyer RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 576+2181+1718, pitcher rows 780+1040+968

Verdict

Meyer's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Michael Harris II should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.360 xwOBA (+45 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +23 pts (bb_type-adj 0.337 -> final 0.360); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Michael Harris II is on a 14-day slump (-114 pts vs season). Max Meyer is in a 14-day rough patch (+47 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Bat-tracking interaction

bat speed 72.8 mph
swing length 8.0 ft
attack angle 6.4°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
Slider-7.8+10.370+2.5on plane
4-Seam Fastball-4.8+1.6236-3.2swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk
Changeup-7.1+11.3107+4.2swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Sweeper-8.4+11.130+2.7on plane
Sinker-6.2+1.392-5.0swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

25.Seiya SuzukiRHBCHC vs Brandon Sproat (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.353 / xBA 0.243 / xSLG 0.347 (+38 pts)Park +28 pts14d 0.282vs RHP 0.301K 26.4%BB 10.8%HR 5.4%Hit 22.5%Slight Hitter

Seiya Suzuki RHB vs Brandon Sproat RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 611+2841+2520, pitcher rows 616+291+0

Verdict

Sproat's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Seiya Suzuki should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.353 xwOBA (+38 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -45 pts (raw 0.339 -> adj 0.293); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +32 pts (bb_type-adj 0.293 -> final 0.353); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Seiya Suzuki is on a 14-day slump (-52 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

26.Harrison BaderRHBSF vs Zac Gallen (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.377 / xBA 0.257 / xSLG 0.462 (+62 pts)Park +15 pts14d —vs RHP —K 24.2%BB 6.4%HR 5.4%Hit 28.4%Edge Hitter

Harrison Bader RHB vs Zac Gallen RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 275+1961+1693, pitcher rows 694+3137+2507

Verdict

Gallen's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Harrison Bader should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.377 xwOBA (+62 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +22 pts (raw 0.320 -> adj 0.342); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +20 pts (bb_type-adj 0.342 -> final 0.377); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %

Pitch Out0.00.3000.322GB 44% / LD 24% / FB 29% / PU 3%0.323+230.3300.2510.46022.4

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

27.Pete AlonsoRHBBAL vs Shane McClanahan (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.315 / xBA 0.230 / xSLG 0.382 (-0 pts)Park -19 pts14d 0.299vs LHP —K 29.0%BB 7.2%HR 5.4%Hit 21.3%Even

Pete Alonso RHB vs Shane McClanahan LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 811+2825+3049, pitcher rows 629+0+0

Verdict

McClanahan's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Pete Alonso should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.315 xwOBA (-0 pts vs league, Even). GB-mix adjustment: -17 pts (raw 0.314 -> adj 0.297); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +37 pts (bb_type-adj 0.297 -> final 0.315); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Shane McClanahan is in a 14-day hot stretch (-65 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

28.Kyle StowersLHBMIA vs JR Ritchie (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.396 / xBA 0.260 / xSLG 0.489 (+81 pts)Park -1 pts14d 0.334vs RHP 0.352K 23.2%BB 20.3%HR 5.3%Hit 18.8%Edge Hitter

Kyle Stowers LHB vs JR Ritchie RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 381+1724+821, pitcher rows 366+0+0

Verdict

Ritchie's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Kyle Stowers should look to drive the curveball. Projection: 0.396 xwOBA (+81 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +23 pts (raw 0.337 -> adj 0.360); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +37 pts (bb_type-adj 0.360 -> final 0.396); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %

Sinker2.40.3330.337GB 32% / LD 30% / FB 32% / PU 6%0.388+550.4100.2780.56616.0

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

29.Mark VientosRHBNYM vs Jake Irvin (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.357 / xBA 0.251 / xSLG 0.411 (+42 pts)Park +20 pts14d 0.464vs RHP 0.437K 21.2%BB 5.7%HR 5.1%Hit 26.7%Slight Hitter

Mark Vientos RHB vs Jake Irvin RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 505+1766+1981, pitcher rows 795+2947+3050

Verdict

Irvin's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Mark Vientos should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.357 xwOBA (+42 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -17 pts (raw 0.329 -> adj 0.313); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +25 pts (bb_type-adj 0.313 -> final 0.357); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Mark Vientos is on a 14-day heater (+95 pts vs season). Jake Irvin is in a 14-day rough patch (+108 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

30.Michael BuschLHBCHC vs Brandon Sproat (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.405 / xBA 0.262 / xSLG 0.465 (+90 pts)Park +41 pts14d 0.439vs RHP 0.484K 23.4%BB 14.9%HR 5.1%Hit 22.3%Edge Hitter

Michael Busch LHB vs Brandon Sproat RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 788+2419+2369, pitcher rows 616+291+0

Verdict

Sproat's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Michael Busch should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.405 xwOBA (+90 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +15 pts (bb_type-adj 0.348 -> final 0.405); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Michael Busch is on a 14-day heater (+103 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

31.Spencer TorkelsonRHBDET vs Slade Cecconi (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.346 / xBA 0.251 / xSLG 0.427 (+31 pts)Park +7 pts14d 0.214vs RHP 0.241K 23.7%BB 5.3%HR 5.1%Hit 24.8%Slight Hitter

Spencer Torkelson RHB vs Slade Cecconi RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 774+2815+1716, pitcher rows 776+2116+1298

Verdict

Cecconi's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Spencer Torkelson should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.346 xwOBA (+31 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -18 pts (raw 0.341 -> adj 0.323); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +16 pts (bb_type-adj 0.323 -> final 0.346); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Spencer Torkelson is on a 14-day slump (-114 pts vs season). Slade Cecconi is in a 14-day hot stretch (-47 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

32.CJ AbramsLHBWSH vs Christian Scott (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.409 / xBA 0.303 / xSLG 0.461 (+94 pts)Park +25 pts14d 0.282vs RHP 0.299K 19.0%BB 8.3%HR 5.1%Hit 28.6%Edge Hitter

CJ Abrams LHB vs Christian Scott RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 739+2324+2273, pitcher rows 288+0+788

Verdict

Scott's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while CJ Abrams should look to drive the slider. Projection: 0.409 xwOBA (+94 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +42 pts (raw 0.327 -> adj 0.370); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +15 pts (bb_type-adj 0.370 -> final 0.409); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. CJ Abrams is on a 14-day slump (-70 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Verdict

Meyer's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Matt Olson should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.357 xwOBA (+42 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +19 pts (bb_type-adj 0.338 -> final 0.357); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Max Meyer is in a 14-day rough patch (+47 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

bat speed 72.5 mph

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

Jorge Soler RHB vs J.T. Ginn RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 712+1291+2388, pitcher rows 668+1580+520

Verdict

Ginn's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Jorge Soler should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.315 xwOBA (+0 pts vs league, Even). Count-state blend: +20 pts (bb_type-adj 0.302 -> final 0.315); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Jorge Soler is on a 14-day slump (-53 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

35.Colton CowserLHBBAL vs Shane McClanahan (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.332 / xBA 0.254 / xSLG 0.382 (+17 pts)Park -23 pts14d —vs LHP —K 28.0%BB 5.8%HR 5.0%Hit 25.9%Even

Colton Cowser LHB vs Shane McClanahan LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 396+1490+2342, pitcher rows 629+0+0

Verdict

McClanahan's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Colton Cowser should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.332 xwOBA (+17 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +46 pts (bb_type-adj 0.309 -> final 0.332); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Shane McClanahan is in a 14-day hot stretch (-65 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

bat speed 69.1 mph
swing length 7.0 ft
attack angle 14.9°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-5.4+10.439+4.9swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

36.Bobby Witt Jr.RHBKC vs Sonny Gray (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.371 / xBA 0.280 / xSLG 0.461 (+56 pts)Park +14 pts14d 0.467vs RHP 0.472K 20.9%BB 4.4%HR 4.9%Hit 29.2%Edge Hitter

Bobby Witt Jr. RHB vs Sonny Gray RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 790+2542+2725, pitcher rows 538+2724+2486

Verdict

Gray's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Bobby Witt Jr. should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.371 xwOBA (+56 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +17 pts (bb_type-adj 0.340 -> final 0.371); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Bobby Witt Jr. is on a 14-day heater (+76 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

37.Willy AdamesRHBSF vs Zac Gallen (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.368 / xBA 0.260 / xSLG 0.449 (+53 pts)Park +14 pts14d 0.310vs RHP 0.326K 25.1%BB 8.9%HR 4.9%Hit 25.1%Edge Hitter

Willy Adames RHB vs Zac Gallen RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 730+2807+2804, pitcher rows 694+3137+2507

Verdict

Gallen's knuckle curve is the projected matchup advantage, while Willy Adames should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.368 xwOBA (+53 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +18 pts (bb_type-adj 0.336 -> final 0.368); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

38.Drake BaldwinLHBATL vs Max Meyer (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.323 / xBA 0.244 / xSLG 0.418 (+8 pts)Park -2 pts14d 0.466vs RHP 0.402K 22.1%BB 8.5%HR 4.9%Hit 21.2%Even

Drake Baldwin LHB vs Max Meyer RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 814+1750+0, pitcher rows 780+1040+968

Verdict

Meyer's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Drake Baldwin should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.323 xwOBA (+8 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -17 pts (raw 0.335 -> adj 0.318); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +7 pts (bb_type-adj 0.318 -> final 0.323); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Drake Baldwin is on a 14-day heater (+56 pts vs season). Max Meyer is in a 14-day rough patch (+47 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

39.Corbin CarrollLHBAZ vs Robbie Ray (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.353 / xBA 0.233 / xSLG 0.459 (+38 pts)Park +15 pts14d 0.441vs LHP —K 21.0%BB 9.4%HR 4.9%Hit 21.9%Slight Hitter

Corbin Carroll LHB vs Robbie Ray LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 710+2514+2649, pitcher rows 868+3072+568

Verdict

Ray's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Corbin Carroll should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.353 xwOBA (+38 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +10 pts (bb_type-adj 0.328 -> final 0.353); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Corbin Carroll is on a 14-day heater (+63 pts vs season). Robbie Ray is in a 14-day rough patch (+85 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

41.Andrew BenintendiLHBCWS vs Bryan Woo (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.290 / xBA 0.234 / xSLG 0.419 (-25 pts)Park -28 pts14d 0.411vs RHP 0.396K 19.0%BB 6.5%HR 4.7%Hit 19.8%Even

Andrew Benintendi LHB vs Bryan Woo RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 551+1729+2001, pitcher rows 770+2784+1717

Verdict

Woo's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Andrew Benintendi should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.290 xwOBA (-25 pts vs league, Pitcher). Count-state blend: +7 pts (bb_type-adj 0.311 -> final 0.290); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Andrew Benintendi is on a 14-day heater (+89 pts vs season). Bryan Woo is in a 14-day hot stretch (-109 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

42.Jo AdellRHBLAA vs J.T. Ginn (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.307 / xBA 0.231 / xSLG 0.375 (-8 pts)Park -10 pts14d 0.390vs RHP 0.351K 31.3%BB 3.5%HR 4.7%Hit 23.2%Even

Jo Adell RHB vs J.T. Ginn RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 747+2221+1845, pitcher rows 668+1580+520

Verdict

Ginn's slider is the projected matchup advantage. Projection: 0.307 xwOBA (-8 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -17 pts (raw 0.304 -> adj 0.287); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +30 pts (bb_type-adj 0.287 -> final 0.307); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Jo Adell is on a 14-day heater (+67 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

William Contreras RHB vs Shota Imanaga LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 655+2710+2744, pitcher rows 841+2222+2594

Verdict

Imanaga's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while William Contreras should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.338 xwOBA (+23 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -7 pts (raw 0.323 -> adj 0.315); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

44.Ketel MarteLHBAZ vs Robbie Ray (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.365 / xBA 0.251 / xSLG 0.449 (+50 pts)Park +16 pts14d 0.298vs LHP —K 17.1%BB 8.1%HR 4.6%Hit 25.3%Slight Hitter

Ketel Marte LHB vs Robbie Ray LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 639+2117+2175, pitcher rows 868+3072+568

Verdict

Ray's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Ketel Marte should look to drive the knuckle curve. Projection: 0.365 xwOBA (+50 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +27 pts (raw 0.308 -> adj 0.336); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +14 pts (bb_type-adj 0.336 -> final 0.365); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Ketel Marte is on a 14-day slump (-47 pts vs season). Robbie Ray is in a 14-day rough patch (+85 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

bat speed 76.5 mph
swing length 8.0 ft
attack angle 6.5°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-4.8+1.096-3.9swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Verdict

Valdez's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Angel Martínez should look to drive the slider. Projection: 0.308 xwOBA (-7 pts vs league, Pitcher). Framber Valdez is in a 14-day rough patch (+66 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

46.JJ BledayLHBCIN vs Andrew Painter (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.326 / xBA 0.249 / xSLG 0.408 (+11 pts)Park +1 pts14d 0.406vs RHP 0.415K 15.9%BB 7.3%HR 4.6%Hit 21.8%Even

JJ Bleday LHB vs Andrew Painter RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 326+1336+2496, pitcher rows 683+0+0

Verdict

Painter's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while JJ Bleday should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.326 xwOBA (+11 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -16 pts (raw 0.337 -> adj 0.321); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Andrew Painter is in a 14-day rough patch (+102 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

48.Carter JensenLHBKC vs Sonny Gray (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.349 / xBA 0.246 / xSLG 0.365 (+34 pts)Park +15 pts14d 0.226vs RHP 0.235K 28.9%BB 7.1%HR 4.5%Hit 25.3%Slight Hitter

Carter Jensen LHB vs Sonny Gray RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 653+293+0, pitcher rows 538+2724+2486

Verdict

Gray's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Carter Jensen should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.349 xwOBA (+34 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -28 pts (raw 0.324 -> adj 0.296); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +38 pts (bb_type-adj 0.296 -> final 0.349); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Carter Jensen is on a 14-day slump (-68 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

attack angle 3.6°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

49.Adolis GarcíaRHBPHI vs Nick Lodolo (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.329 / xBA 0.211 / xSLG 0.392 (+14 pts)Park +4 pts14d 0.279vs LHP —K 27.9%BB 4.7%HR 4.5%Hit 24.4%Even

Adolis García RHB vs Nick Lodolo LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 760+2154+2542, pitcher rows 165+2497+1865

Verdict

Lodolo's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Adolis García should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.329 xwOBA (+14 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -10 pts (raw 0.299 -> adj 0.289); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +36 pts (bb_type-adj 0.289 -> final 0.329); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Gray's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Vinnie Pasquantino should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.332 xwOBA (+17 pts vs league, Hitter). Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

51.Jazz Chisholm Jr.LHBNYY vs Patrick Corbin (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.358 / xBA 0.287 / xSLG 0.432 (+43 pts)Park -8 pts14d 0.246vs LHP —K 23.4%BB 8.3%HR 4.5%Hit 26.1%Slight Hitter

Jazz Chisholm Jr. LHB vs Patrick Corbin LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 734+2253+2760, pitcher rows 539+2590+2889

Verdict

Corbin's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Jazz Chisholm Jr. should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.358 xwOBA (+43 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +34 pts (raw 0.310 -> adj 0.344); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +22 pts (bb_type-adj 0.344 -> final 0.358); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Verdict

Gallen's knuckle curve is the projected matchup advantage, while Casey Schmitt should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.356 xwOBA (+41 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +16 pts (bb_type-adj 0.329 -> final 0.356); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Pitch Out0.00.3330.322GB 44% / LD 24% / FB 29% / PU 3%0.332-10.3390.2730.46619.4

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Coverage % = share of the pitcher's attack on this pitch landing in zones with batter data; remainder imputed at league baselines (xwOBA 0.315, xBA 0.245, xSLG 0.405) so two batters with different coverage maps stay comparable.

Bat-tracking interaction

bat speed 69.5 mph
swing length 7.5 ft
attack angle 9.7°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-5.1+4.196-1.0on plane
Knuckle Curve-10.1+17.88+7.7swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Slider-7.6+14.965+7.3swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Changeup-7.6+18.810+11.1swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Sinker-5.6+4.368-1.2on plane
Cutter-7.4+9.733+2.3on plane

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

53.Drew GilbertLHBSF vs Zac Gallen (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.371 / xBA 0.252 / xSLG 0.453 (+56 pts)Park +11 pts14d 0.229vs RHP 0.256K 17.7%BB 7.0%HR 4.4%Hit 26.2%Edge Hitter

Drew Gilbert LHB vs Zac Gallen RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 346+416+0, pitcher rows 694+3137+2507

Verdict

Gallen's knuckle curve is the projected matchup advantage, while Drew Gilbert should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.371 xwOBA (+56 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +18 pts (raw 0.320 -> adj 0.338); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +22 pts (bb_type-adj 0.338 -> final 0.371); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

54.Luis RengifoRHBMIL vs Shota Imanaga (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.322 / xBA 0.203 / xSLG 0.451 (+7 pts)Park +31 pts14d 0.301vs LHP —K 20.7%BB 2.8%HR 4.4%Hit 25.7%Even

Luis Rengifo RHB vs Shota Imanaga LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 522+2011+1086, pitcher rows 841+2222+2594

Verdict

Imanaga's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Luis Rengifo should look to drive the curveball. Projection: 0.322 xwOBA (+7 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -11 pts (raw 0.301 -> adj 0.290); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

55.Samuel BasalloLHBBAL vs Shane McClanahan (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.266 / xBA 0.243 / xSLG 0.403 (-49 pts)Park -15 pts14d 0.369vs LHP —K 22.9%BB 8.3%HR 4.4%Hit 18.0%Slight Pitcher

Samuel Basallo LHB vs Shane McClanahan LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 501+443+0, pitcher rows 629+0+0

Verdict

McClanahan's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Samuel Basallo should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.266 xwOBA (-49 pts vs league, Pitcher). Count-state blend: -33 pts (bb_type-adj 0.314 -> final 0.266); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Shane McClanahan is in a 14-day hot stretch (-65 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

56.Yandy DíazRHBTB vs Trevor Rogers (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.302 / xBA 0.242 / xSLG 0.437 (-13 pts)Park -18 pts14d 0.322vs LHP —K 13.3%BB 7.6%HR 4.3%Hit 20.2%Even

Yandy Díaz RHB vs Trevor Rogers LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 680+2543+2461, pitcher rows 521+1627+2248

Verdict

Rogers' sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Yandy Díaz should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.302 xwOBA (-13 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -14 pts (raw 0.334 -> adj 0.320); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Painter's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Sal Stewart should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.321 xwOBA (+6 pts vs league, Hitter). window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 829+2018+2437, pitcher rows 616+291+0

Verdict

Sproat's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Alex Bregman should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.336 xwOBA (+21 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -26 pts (raw 0.322 -> adj 0.296); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
Sinker39.90.3240.319GB 59% / LD 20% / FB 8% / PU 12%0.308-160.3130.2650.3512.1

Curveball6.00.3030.328GB 48% / LD 23% / FB 19% / PU 9%0.295-80.3080.2500.39913.1

Slider1.50.3010.314GB 48% / LD 23% / FB 19% / PU 9%0.291-100.2900.2380.36238.7

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

59.Nathaniel LoweLHBCIN vs Andrew Painter (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.338 / xBA 0.241 / xSLG 0.396 (+23 pts)Park +4 pts14d 0.319vs RHP 0.327K 17.7%BB 6.8%HR 4.3%Hit 25.2%Even

Nathaniel Lowe LHB vs Andrew Painter RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 440+2640+2361, pitcher rows 683+0+0

Verdict

Painter's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Nathaniel Lowe should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.338 xwOBA (+23 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -21 pts (raw 0.331 -> adj 0.310); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +24 pts (bb_type-adj 0.310 -> final 0.338); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Andrew Painter is in a 14-day rough patch (+102 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Ben Rice LHB vs Patrick Corbin LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 713+2129+725, pitcher rows 539+2590+2889

Verdict

Corbin's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Ben Rice should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.305 xwOBA (-10 pts vs league, Pitcher). Ben Rice is on a 14-day slump (-96 pts vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

61.Blake PerkinsRHBMIL vs Shota Imanaga (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.361 / xBA 0.233 / xSLG 0.479 (+46 pts)Park +53 pts14d —vs LHP —K 20.6%BB 4.7%HR 4.3%Hit 27.1%Slight Hitter

Blake Perkins RHB vs Shota Imanaga LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 241+807+1773, pitcher rows 841+2222+2594

Verdict

Imanaga's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Blake Perkins should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.361 xwOBA (+46 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +20 pts (raw 0.304 -> adj 0.324); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -15 pts (bb_type-adj 0.324 -> final 0.361); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

bat speed 67.9 mph
swing length 7.3 ft

PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-4.2+4.464+0.3on plane
Split-Finger-7.2+5.6*2-1.5on plane

Slider-7.1+9.211+2.1on plane

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).
