Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

62.Christian WalkerRHBHOU vs Kendry Rojas (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.326 / xBA 0.261 / xSLG 0.408 (+11 pts)Park -5 pts14d 0.249vs LHP —K 19.3%BB 9.6%HR 4.2%Hit 22.2%Even

Christian Walker RHB vs Kendry Rojas LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 711+2519+2326, pitcher rows 136+0+0

Verdict

Rojas' 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Christian Walker should look to drive the slider. Projection: 0.326 xwOBA (+11 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +14 pts (raw 0.310 -> adj 0.324); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +7 pts (bb_type-adj 0.324 -> final 0.326); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Christian Walker is on a 14-day slump (-88 pts vs season). Kendry Rojas is in a 14-day hot stretch (-48 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

63.Max MuncyLHBLAD vs Michael King (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.322 / xBA 0.247 / xSLG 0.418 (+7 pts)Park -23 pts14d 0.385vs RHP 0.419K 24.0%BB 15.9%HR 4.2%Hit 16.4%Even

Max Muncy LHB vs Michael King RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 696+1879+1447, pitcher rows 831+1238+2993

Verdict

King's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Max Muncy should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.322 xwOBA (+7 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -21 pts (raw 0.351 -> adj 0.329); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +16 pts (bb_type-adj 0.329 -> final 0.322); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

64.Matt ChapmanRHBSF vs Zac Gallen (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.383 / xBA 0.256 / xSLG 0.449 (+68 pts)Park +16 pts14d 0.205vs RHP 0.206K 25.1%BB 9.8%HR 4.2%Hit 27.1%Edge Hitter

Matt Chapman RHB vs Zac Gallen RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 760+2213+2722, pitcher rows 694+3137+2507

Verdict

Gallen's knuckle curve is the projected matchup advantage, while Matt Chapman should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.383 xwOBA (+68 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +16 pts (raw 0.325 -> adj 0.341); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +26 pts (bb_type-adj 0.341 -> final 0.383); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

65.Keibert RuizLHBWSH vs Christian Scott (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.378 / xBA 0.305 / xSLG 0.448 (+63 pts)Park +17 pts14d 0.313vs RHP —K 15.4%BB 5.2%HR 4.1%Hit 29.9%Edge Hitter

Keibert Ruiz LHB vs Christian Scott RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 309+889+1662, pitcher rows 288+0+788

Verdict

Scott's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Keibert Ruiz should look to drive the split-finger. Projection: 0.378 xwOBA (+63 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +43 pts (raw 0.315 -> adj 0.358); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Keibert Ruiz is on a 14-day heater (+62 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Projected slashxwOBAxBAxSLG

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

bat speed 65.4 mph

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

66.Rafael DeversLHBSF vs Zac Gallen (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.360 / xBA 0.246 / xSLG 0.431 (+45 pts)Park +14 pts14d 0.267vs RHP 0.279K 22.1%BB 11.6%HR 4.1%Hit 24.5%Slight Hitter

Rafael Devers LHB vs Zac Gallen RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 774+3102+2433, pitcher rows 694+3137+2507

Verdict

Gallen's knuckle curve is the projected matchup advantage, while Rafael Devers should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.360 xwOBA (+45 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +16 pts (bb_type-adj 0.330 -> final 0.360); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

67.Brady HouseRHBWSH vs Christian Scott (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.341 / xBA 0.212 / xSLG 0.384 (+26 pts)Park +22 pts14d 0.345vs RHP 0.333K 28.3%BB 5.1%HR 4.1%Hit 27.0%Slight Hitter

Brady House RHB vs Christian Scott RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 629+969+0, pitcher rows 288+0+788

Verdict

Scott's 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage. Projection: 0.341 xwOBA (+26 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -8 pts (raw 0.304 -> adj 0.295); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +23 pts (bb_type-adj 0.295 -> final 0.341); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

68.José RamírezLHBCLE vs Framber Valdez (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.323 / xBA 0.255 / xSLG 0.348 (+8 pts)Park +6 pts14d 0.371vs LHP —K 16.6%BB 4.8%HR 4.1%Hit 25.5%Even

José Ramírez LHB vs Framber Valdez LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 875+2816+2971, pitcher rows 800+2898+2683

Verdict

Valdez's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while José Ramírez should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.323 xwOBA (+8 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -15 pts (raw 0.311 -> adj 0.296); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +21 pts (bb_type-adj 0.296 -> final 0.323); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Framber Valdez is in a 14-day rough patch (+66 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

69.Mike YastrzemskiLHBATL vs Max Meyer (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.338 / xBA 0.257 / xSLG 0.425 (+23 pts)Park -3 pts14d 0.352vs RHP —K 23.6%BB 9.7%HR 4.1%Hit 22.7%Even

Mike Yastrzemski LHB vs Max Meyer RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 553+2321+1960, pitcher rows 780+1040+968

Verdict

Meyer's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Mike Yastrzemski should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.338 xwOBA (+23 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +12 pts (bb_type-adj 0.328 -> final 0.338); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Mike Yastrzemski is on a 14-day heater (+76 pts vs season). Max Meyer is in a 14-day rough patch (+47 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

swing length 7.4 ft

PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
Slider-7.8+14.165+6.3swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

70.Cody BellingerLHBNYY vs Patrick Corbin (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.330 / xBA 0.286 / xSLG 0.405 (+15 pts)Park -11 pts14d 0.390vs LHP —K 14.2%BB 9.2%HR 4.0%Hit 22.7%Even

Cody Bellinger LHB vs Patrick Corbin LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 737+2697+2109, pitcher rows 539+2590+2889

Verdict

Corbin's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Cody Bellinger should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.330 xwOBA (+15 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +10 pts (raw 0.324 -> adj 0.333); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +8 pts (bb_type-adj 0.333 -> final 0.330); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

71.Daylen LileLHBWSH vs Christian Scott (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.379 / xBA 0.291 / xSLG 0.421 (+64 pts)Park +17 pts14d 0.390vs RHP 0.423K 18.3%BB 8.2%HR 4.0%Hit 26.7%Edge Hitter

Daylen Lile LHB vs Christian Scott RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 684+1306+0, pitcher rows 288+0+788

Verdict

Scott's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Daylen Lile should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.379 xwOBA (+64 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +11 pts (raw 0.332 -> adj 0.343); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +19 pts (bb_type-adj 0.343 -> final 0.379); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Daylen Lile is on a 14-day heater (+47 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Verdict

Cecconi's cutter is the projected matchup advantage, while Matt Vierling should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.346 xwOBA (+31 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +9 pts (bb_type-adj 0.329 -> final 0.346); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Matt Vierling is on a 14-day heater (+62 pts vs season). Slade Cecconi is in a 14-day hot stretch (-47 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

73.Miguel VargasRHBCWS vs Bryan Woo (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.248 / xBA 0.220 / xSLG 0.347 (-67 pts)Park -20 pts14d 0.525vs RHP 0.496K 23.2%BB 3.8%HR 4.0%Hit 17.3%Edge Pitcher

Miguel Vargas RHB vs Bryan Woo RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 781+2326+1009, pitcher rows 770+2784+1717

Verdict

Woo's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Miguel Vargas should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.248 xwOBA (-67 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -31 pts (raw 0.308 -> adj 0.278); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -9 pts (bb_type-adj 0.278 -> final 0.248); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Miguel Vargas is on a 14-day heater (+117 pts vs season). Bryan Woo is in a 14-day hot stretch (-109 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
Sinker38.70.3660.318GB 66% / LD 22% / FB 10% / PU 2%0.294-730.2960.2700.3482.3
4-Seam Fastball38.30.3230.269GB 20% / LD 25% / FB 38% / PU 17%0.320-30.2740.1790.35727.2
Sweeper14.50.3040.242GB 32% / LD 24% / FB 34% / PU 9%0.301-30.2290.1900.28434.8

Changeup1.00.3280.316GB 46% / LD 23% / FB 23% / PU 8%0.311-170.3120.2510.39213.2

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

75.Luis García Jr.LHBWSH vs Christian Scott (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.384 / xBA 0.294 / xSLG 0.462 (+69 pts)Park +23 pts14d 0.403vs RHP 0.388K 17.3%BB 6.2%HR 3.8%Hit 30.3%Edge Hitter

Luis García Jr. LHB vs Christian Scott RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 497+1842+1890, pitcher rows 288+0+788

Verdict

Scott's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Luis García Jr. should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.384 xwOBA (+69 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +15 pts (raw 0.341 -> adj 0.356); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Luis García Jr. is on a 14-day heater (+77 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
4-Seam Fastball55.30.3500.316GB 37% / LD 38% / FB 19% / PU 6%0.368+190.3690.3130.46910.0
Cutter15.00.3380.315GB 35% / LD 33% / FB 25% / PU 8%0.345+60.3450.2660.4461.3

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

76.Yoán MoncadaLHBLAA vs J.T. Ginn (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.331 / xBA 0.242 / xSLG 0.372 (+16 pts)Park -6 pts14d —vs RHP —K 20.1%BB 11.7%HR 3.7%Hit 19.5%Even

Yoán Moncada LHB vs J.T. Ginn RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 521+1134+192, pitcher rows 668+1580+520

Verdict

Ginn's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Yoán Moncada should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.331 xwOBA (+16 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -30 pts (raw 0.333 -> adj 0.303); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +33 pts (bb_type-adj 0.303 -> final 0.331); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

77.Elly De La CruzLHBCIN vs Andrew Painter (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.350 / xBA 0.248 / xSLG 0.450 (+35 pts)14d 0.391vs RHP 0.419K 19.6%BB 6.3%HR 3.7%Hit 25.5%Slight Hitter

Elly De La Cruz LHB vs Andrew Painter RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 821+2763+2762, pitcher rows 683+0+0

Verdict

Painter's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Elly De La Cruz should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.350 xwOBA (+35 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -14 pts (raw 0.346 -> adj 0.332); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +18 pts (bb_type-adj 0.332 -> final 0.350); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Andrew Painter is in a 14-day rough patch (+102 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

78.Mike TroutRHBLAA vs J.T. Ginn (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.298 / xBA 0.226 / xSLG 0.366 (-17 pts)Park -6 pts14d 0.296vs RHP 0.347K 32.5%BB 8.2%HR 3.7%Hit 20.5%Even

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 875+2511+578, pitcher rows 668+1580+520

Verdict

Ginn's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Mike Trout should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.298 xwOBA (-17 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -32 pts (raw 0.320 -> adj 0.289); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +15 pts (bb_type-adj 0.289 -> final 0.298); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Mike Trout is on a 14-day slump (-102 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

79.Manny MachadoRHBSD vs Yoshinobu Yamamoto (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.290 / xBA 0.227 / xSLG 0.395 (-25 pts)Park -17 pts14d 0.259vs RHP 0.261K 25.9%BB 5.8%HR 3.7%Hit 21.3%Even

Manny Machado RHB vs Yoshinobu Yamamoto RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 750+2714+2490, pitcher rows 665+3245+1713

Verdict

Yamamoto's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Manny Machado should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.290 xwOBA (-25 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -19 pts (raw 0.316 -> adj 0.297); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +10 pts (bb_type-adj 0.297 -> final 0.290); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Manny Machado is on a 14-day slump (-43 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

80.Dalton RushingLHBLAD vs Michael King (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.312 / xBA 0.240 / xSLG 0.407 (-3 pts)Park -22 pts14d 0.249vs RHP —K 29.4%BB 8.3%HR 3.7%Hit 20.9%Even

Dalton Rushing LHB vs Michael King RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 286+604+0, pitcher rows 831+1238+2993

Verdict

King's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Dalton Rushing should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.312 xwOBA (-3 pts vs league, Even). GB-mix adjustment: +6 pts (raw 0.308 -> adj 0.313); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +20 pts (bb_type-adj 0.313 -> final 0.312); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Dalton Rushing is on a 14-day slump (-113 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

81.Spencer SteerRHBCIN vs Andrew Painter (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.345 / xBA 0.245 / xSLG 0.424 (+30 pts)Park +2 pts14d 0.428vs RHP 0.380K 21.1%BB 10.0%HR 3.6%Hit 23.3%Slight Hitter

Spencer Steer RHB vs Andrew Painter RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 669+2347+2630, pitcher rows 683+0+0

Verdict

Painter's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Spencer Steer should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.345 xwOBA (+30 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +20 pts (bb_type-adj 0.323 -> final 0.345); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Spencer Steer is on a 14-day heater (+46 pts vs season). Andrew Painter is in a 14-day rough patch (+102 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

82.Gavin SheetsLHBSD vs Yoshinobu Yamamoto (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.281 / xBA 0.218 / xSLG 0.341 (-34 pts)Park -21 pts14d 0.462vs RHP 0.471K 24.0%BB 7.8%HR 3.6%Hit 19.0%Slight Pitcher

Gavin Sheets LHB vs Yoshinobu Yamamoto RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 583+2301+2094, pitcher rows 665+3245+1713

Verdict

Yamamoto's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Gavin Sheets should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.281 xwOBA (-34 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -22 pts (raw 0.300 -> adj 0.277); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +24 pts (bb_type-adj 0.277 -> final 0.281); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Gavin Sheets is on a 14-day heater (+99 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

Bat-tracking interaction

bat speed 72.6 mph
swing length 7.7 ft
attack angle 7.9°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-4.5+1.8223-2.7on plane
Split-Finger-7.2+11.339+4.1swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Curveball-10.1+13.952+3.8swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Cutter-5.5+8.665+3.1swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Sinker-4.7+2.461-2.3on plane
Slider-7.3+13.086+5.7swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

83.Ceddanne RafaelaRHBBOS vs Seth Lugo (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.355 / xBA 0.260 / xSLG 0.435 (+40 pts)Park +13 pts14d 0.322vs RHP 0.301K 19.2%BB 4.5%HR 3.6%Hit 28.1%Slight Hitter

Ceddanne Rafaela RHB vs Seth Lugo RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 605+2180+2074, pitcher rows 851+2381+3285

Verdict

Lugo's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Ceddanne Rafaela should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.355 xwOBA (+40 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +16 pts (raw 0.311 -> adj 0.327); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +15 pts (bb_type-adj 0.327 -> final 0.355); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Seth Lugo is in a 14-day rough patch (+92 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. 84.Tyrone TaylorRHBNYM vs Jake Irvin (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.352 / xBA 0.247 / xSLG 0.406 (+37 pts)Park +20 pts14d —vs RHP —K 18.4%BB 3.9%HR 3.6%Hit 27.7%Slight Hitter

Tyrone Taylor RHB vs Jake Irvin RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 308+1251+1486, pitcher rows 795+2947+3050

Verdict

Irvin's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Tyrone Taylor should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.352 xwOBA (+37 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -10 pts (raw 0.320 -> adj 0.309); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +22 pts (bb_type-adj 0.309 -> final 0.352); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Jake Irvin is in a 14-day rough patch (+108 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

Verdict

Painter's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Blake Dunn should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.313 xwOBA (-2 pts vs league, Even). GB-mix adjustment: -12 pts (raw 0.322 -> adj 0.310); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Andrew Painter is in a 14-day rough patch (+102 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

86.Ozzie AlbiesLHBATL vs Max Meyer (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.324 / xBA 0.258 / xSLG 0.420 (+9 pts)Park -5 pts14d 0.227vs RHP 0.245K 20.0%BB 8.5%HR 3.5%Hit 23.5%Even

Ozzie Albies LHB vs Max Meyer RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 721+2411+1686, pitcher rows 780+1040+968

Verdict

Meyer's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Ozzie Albies should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.324 xwOBA (+9 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +13 pts (raw 0.314 -> adj 0.326); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Ozzie Albies is on a 14-day slump (-69 pts vs season). Max Meyer is in a 14-day rough patch (+47 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Pitch-mix projection

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
Slider32.50.3080.306GB 48% / LD 20% / FB 30% / PU 2%0.307-10.2970.2310.38337.5
4-Seam Fastball29.60.3210.345GB 30% / LD 28% / FB 31% / PU 12%0.347+260.3780.2810.5046.7
Changeup24.20.2900.316GB 60% / LD 25% / FB 14% / PU 1%0.305+150.3060.2720.38118.6
Sweeper9.70.3140.302GB 44% / LD 25% / FB 27% / PU 4%0.325+110.3130.2410.39142.3
Sinker3.90.3210.318GB 44% / LD 25% / FB 27% / PU 4%0.335+130.3370.2770.4179.6

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

88.Andy PagesRHBLAD vs Michael King (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.305 / xBA 0.239 / xSLG 0.389 (-10 pts)Park -17 pts14d 0.381vs RHP 0.419K 25.6%BB 5.4%HR 3.5%Hit 22.1%Even

Andy Pages RHB vs Michael King RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 767+2543+1810, pitcher rows 831+1238+2993

Verdict

King's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Andy Pages should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.305 xwOBA (-10 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -9 pts (raw 0.310 -> adj 0.301); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +21 pts (bb_type-adj 0.301 -> final 0.305); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

89.Nolan ArenadoRHBAZ vs Robbie Ray (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.329 / xBA 0.244 / xSLG 0.428 (+14 pts)Park +12 pts14d 0.398vs LHP —K 18.1%BB 8.8%HR 3.5%Hit 23.8%Even

Nolan Arenado RHB vs Robbie Ray LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 652+1603+2463, pitcher rows 868+3072+568

Verdict

Ray's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Nolan Arenado should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.329 xwOBA (+14 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: -6 pts (bb_type-adj 0.323 -> final 0.329); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Nolan Arenado is on a 14-day heater (+66 pts vs season). Robbie Ray is in a 14-day rough patch (+85 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

bat speed 70.3 mph
swing length 8.3 ft

PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

90.Teoscar HernándezRHBLAD vs Michael King (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.317 / xBA 0.234 / xSLG 0.412 (+2 pts)Park -21 pts14d 0.335vs RHP 0.304K 30.4%BB 6.6%HR 3.5%Hit 23.7%Even

Teoscar Hernández RHB vs Michael King RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 676+2399+2845, pitcher rows 831+1238+2993

Verdict

King's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Teoscar Hernández should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.317 xwOBA (+2 pts vs league, Even). GB-mix adjustment: -8 pts (raw 0.319 -> adj 0.311); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +28 pts (bb_type-adj 0.311 -> final 0.317); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

91.Josh JungRHBTEX vs Jose Quintana (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.360 / xBA 0.274 / xSLG 0.454 (+45 pts)Park +10 pts14d 0.300vs LHP —K 13.4%BB 7.9%HR 3.5%Hit 27.8%Slight Hitter

Josh Jung RHB vs Jose Quintana LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 613+1931+693, pitcher rows 562+2275+3093

Verdict

Quintana's slurve is the projected matchup advantage, while Josh Jung should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.360 xwOBA (+45 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +10 pts (raw 0.334 -> adj 0.344); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +6 pts (bb_type-adj 0.344 -> final 0.360); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Josh Jung is on a 14-day slump (-55 pts vs season). Jose Quintana is in a 14-day hot stretch (-50 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. 92.Brett BatyLHBNYM vs Jake Irvin (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.431 / xBA 0.257 / xSLG 0.514 (+116 pts)Park +33 pts14d 0.334vs RHP 0.374K 19.0%BB 9.8%HR 3.5%Hit 33.4%Strong Hitter

Brett Baty LHB vs Jake Irvin RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 601+1757+681, pitcher rows 795+2947+3050

Verdict

Irvin's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Brett Baty should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.431 xwOBA (+116 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +7 pts (raw 0.358 -> adj 0.364); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +34 pts (bb_type-adj 0.364 -> final 0.431); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Jake Irvin is in a 14-day rough patch (+108 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

93.Dansby SwansonRHBCHC vs Brandon Sproat (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.354 / xBA 0.245 / xSLG 0.358 (+39 pts)Park +30 pts14d 0.221vs RHP 0.217K 24.6%BB 10.2%HR 3.5%Hit 25.0%Slight Hitter

Dansby Swanson RHB vs Brandon Sproat RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 713+2625+2365, pitcher rows 616+291+0

Verdict

Sproat's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Dansby Swanson should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.354 xwOBA (+39 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -33 pts (raw 0.331 -> adj 0.299); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +24 pts (bb_type-adj 0.299 -> final 0.354); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Dansby Swanson is on a 14-day slump (-90 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

94.Tyler StephensonRHBCIN vs Andrew Painter (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.365 / xBA 0.243 / xSLG 0.452 (+50 pts)Park +8 pts14d 0.252vs RHP 0.256K 27.1%BB 10.1%HR 3.4%Hit 26.0%Edge Hitter

Tyler Stephenson RHB vs Andrew Painter RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 499+1424+1956, pitcher rows 683+0+0

Verdict

Painter's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Tyler Stephenson should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.365 xwOBA (+50 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +26 pts (bb_type-adj 0.330 -> final 0.365); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Tyler Stephenson is on a 14-day slump (-58 pts vs season). Andrew Painter is in a 14-day rough patch (+102 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

95.Julio RodríguezRHBSEA vs Noah Schultz (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.327 / xBA 0.245 / xSLG 0.419 (+12 pts)Park -17 pts14d 0.309vs LHP —K 19.4%BB 15.6%HR 3.4%Hit 18.6%Even

Julio Rodríguez RHB vs Noah Schultz LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 808+2727+2389, pitcher rows 491+0+0

Verdict

Schultz's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Julio Rodríguez should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.327 xwOBA (+12 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: -8 pts (raw 0.330 -> adj 0.322); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +22 pts (bb_type-adj 0.322 -> final 0.327); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Noah Schultz is in a 14-day rough patch (+115 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
4-Seam Fastball29.60.3450.323GB 41% / LD 27% / FB 20% / PU 12%0.318-280.3260.2470.41122.4
Sweeper22.30.3030.310GB 41% / LD 26% / FB 22% / PU 11%0.282-220.2770.2170.34330.1

Cutter16.60.3090.337GB 41% / LD 26% / FB 22% / PU 11%0.290-200.3110.2450.40013.8
Changeup9.20.3300.306GB 41% / LD 26% / FB 22% / PU 11%0.328-20.3200.2430.44255.2

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Coverage % = share of the pitcher's attack on this pitch landing in zones with batter data; remainder imputed at league baselines (xwOBA 0.315, xBA 0.245, xSLG 0.405) so two batters with different coverage maps stay comparable.

Bat-tracking interaction

bat speed 75.2 mph
swing length 7.6 ft
attack angle 6.3°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-4.9-0.7108-5.6swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk
Sinker-5.8-1.729-7.5swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk
Sweeper-7.9+15.918+8.0swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Cutter-5.9+4.229-1.6on plane
Changeup-7.2+11.669+4.4swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

96.Matt McLainRHBCIN vs Andrew Painter (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.350 / xBA 0.245 / xSLG 0.420 (+35 pts)Park +8 pts14d 0.383vs RHP 0.350K 24.5%BB 9.9%HR 3.4%Hit 24.9%Slight Hitter

Matt McLain RHB vs Andrew Painter RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 741+2402+0, pitcher rows 683+0+0

Verdict

Painter's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Matt McLain should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.350 xwOBA (+35 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +5 pts (raw 0.313 -> adj 0.319); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +23 pts (bb_type-adj 0.319 -> final 0.350); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Matt McLain is on a 14-day heater (+56 pts vs season). Andrew Painter is in a 14-day rough patch (+102 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

97.Randy ArozarenaRHBSEA vs Noah Schultz (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.315 / xBA 0.259 / xSLG 0.397 (+0 pts)Park -19 pts14d 0.305vs LHP —K 19.7%BB 16.8%HR 3.4%Hit 16.2%Even

Randy Arozarena RHB vs Noah Schultz LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 806+3112+2776, pitcher rows 491+0+0

Verdict

Schultz's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Randy Arozarena should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.315 xwOBA (+0 pts vs league, Even). GB-mix adjustment: -6 pts (raw 0.328 -> adj 0.322); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +13 pts (bb_type-adj 0.322 -> final 0.315); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Noah Schultz is in a 14-day rough patch (+115 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

Yamamoto's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Fernando Tatis Jr. should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.325 xwOBA (+10 pts vs league, Hitter). Count-state blend: +10 pts (bb_type-adj 0.335 -> final 0.325); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Fernando Tatis Jr. is on a 14-day slump (-41 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

swing length 7.2 ft

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Pitch-mix projection

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
4-Seam Fastball53.20.3220.340GB 30% / LD 27% / FB 35% / PU 8%0.330+80.3550.2680.48515.5
Curveball24.80.3050.293GB 46% / LD 32% / FB 19% / PU 2%0.315+100.2930.2360.35940.2
Changeup14.00.3150.293GB 44% / LD 24% / FB 24% / PU 8%0.303-120.2800.1970.37955.8
Cutter7.40.3260.315GB 45% / LD 26% / FB 22% / PU 6%0.319-80.3180.2420.39936.0
Slider0.30.3140.316GB 37% / LD 28% / FB 29% / PU 6%0.308-60.3090.2430.38032.3
Sinker0.30.3250.320GB 37% / LD 28% / FB 29% / PU 6%0.356+300.3600.2680.5020.0

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

100.Michael ConfortoLHBCHC vs Brandon Sproat (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.396 / xBA 0.258 / xSLG 0.444 (+81 pts)Park +35 pts14d 0.490vs RHP 0.474K 23.4%BB 14.8%HR 3.3%Hit 24.4%Edge Hitter

Michael Conforto LHB vs Brandon Sproat RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 277+2022+1969, pitcher rows 616+291+0

Verdict

Sproat's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Michael Conforto should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.396 xwOBA (+81 pts vs league, Hitter). GB-mix adjustment: +14 pts (raw 0.326 -> adj 0.341); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +20 pts (bb_type-adj 0.341 -> final 0.396); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Michael Conforto is on a 14-day heater (+43 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

Coverage % = share of the pitcher's attack on this pitch landing in zones with batter data; remainder imputed at league baselines (xwOBA 0.315, xBA 0.245, xSLG 0.405) so two batters with different coverage maps stay comparable.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).
